title: Investigate and fix API key Authorization header format
description: Integration tests fail with 'invalid token' when using api_key auth. The current header is 'Authorization: token <key>' but Orpheus/Redacted may expect a different format (e.g. bare key or different prefix). Needs investigation against live API docs or trial-and-error during fixture capture.
notes: Root cause confirmed: API-key Authorization header is per-tracker. Orpheus wants 'token <key>' (Gazelle recommended); RED wants the bare key (no prefix). Fix: added api_key_prefix param to GazelleTransport (default 'token '), RedactedClient sets it to ''. Also discovered during live verification: (1) RED migrated redacted.ch -> redacted.sh (old domain returns 410); updated REDACTED_BASE_URL. (2) RED rejects requests lacking a custom User-Agent; added default UA 'python-gazelle'. (3) Orpheus omits userstats.requiredRatio -> made UserStats.required_ratio Optional. Orpheus auth VERIFIED live (user.me OK, id=REDACTED). RED still returns 401 'bad credentials' = key value issue (likely rotated key in .env), NOT a code bug; awaiting valid key to confirm.
close_reason: Fixed per-tracker API-key auth: Orpheus uses 'token <key>', RED uses bare key (api_key_prefix param). Also fixed RED domain redacted.ch->redacted.sh, added default User-Agent (RED 401s without one), made UserStats.required_ratio optional (Orpheus omits it). Both trackers verified live: Orpheus id=REDACTED, RED id=REDACTED.

title: Task 12: Integration tests
description: Set up tests/conftest.py with load_dotenv(). Integration conftest with skip-if-missing credential fixtures. Integration tests for OrpheusClient and RedactedClient against live APIs (read-only, both auth methods). Finalize __init__.py public exports. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Integration tests, public exports, and security fixes committed

title: Task 11: API fixtures and model tests
description: Write devtools/capture_fixtures.py to fetch real API responses from Orpheus and Redacted. Write fixture-driven model tests validating Pydantic models against real responses. Fix any field mismatches. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 10: Sync wrapper
description: Implement _BackgroundLoop (daemon thread + persistent event loop), _SyncProxy (__getattr__ wrapping async methods), GazelleSyncClient, OrpheusClientSync, RedactedClientSync. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 9: GazelleClient and tracker subclasses
description: Implement GazelleClient composing transport + resource properties. OrpheusClient and RedactedClient subclasses with correct base URLs. Async context manager support. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 8: Resource layer
description: Implement BaseResource and all 7 resources: TorrentResource (get/search/download), ArtistResource (get/search), RequestResource, CollageResource, UserResource, InboxResource, NotificationResource. Unit tests with StubTransport. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 7: Pydantic v2 response models
description: Implement GazelleModel base with camelCase alias_generator and extra=ignore. Implement all resource models: Torrent/TorrentGroup/TorrentResult, Artist/ArtistResult, Request/RequestResult, Collage, User/UserStats, Message, Notification. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 6: Transport — retry and error mapping
description: Implement exponential backoff retry for 429 and 5xx. Map status codes to typed GazelleError subclasses. Non-retryable: 400/401/403/404. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 5: Token-bucket rate limiter
description: Implement TokenBucket class using asyncio.Lock and asyncio.sleep. Wire into GazelleTransport.request(). Default 3 req/s, configurable. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 4: Transport — authentication
description: Implement cookie/session auth (login POST, session cookie) and API key auth (Authorization header) with auto re-auth on 401/403. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Transport — core request and JSON parsing
description: Implement GazelleTransport with httpx.AsyncClient, base URL, Gazelle JSON response parsing, and MockTransport test helper. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Error hierarchy
description: Implement GazelleError, GazelleAuthError, GazelleRateLimitError, GazelleNotFoundError, GazelleAPIError in errors.py with TDD. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Project setup — deps and module skeleton
description: Add httpx, pydantic, pytest-asyncio, python-dotenv to pyproject.toml. Configure asyncio_mode=auto. Create all module skeleton files. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Closed

title: Orpheus & Redacted tracker adapters
description: Tracker-specific adapter layer handling API quirks and endpoint signature differences between Orpheus and Redacted. Includes integration test suite validating both trackers. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. OrpheusClient/RedactedClient with per-tracker URL + auth-header divergence; 4 adapter unit tests; integration suite (test_orpheus/test_redacted, 4 each); divergences via nmq + 6ue.

title: Gazelle endpoint coverage
description: Typed wrappers for all standard Gazelle endpoints: torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Task 7 (ecm) + Task 8 (1bj). All seven endpoint namespaces have typed wrappers: torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications. Core read methods per group; exhaustive breadth can be a future issue.

title: Authentication & session management
description: Cookie/session-based auth and API key support. Handle login, session persistence, and re-auth on expiry. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Delivered via Task 4 (6dq) + bug nmq. transport.py: api_key auth, cookie login with keeplogged=1 session persistence (_login), re-auth on 401 expiry (transport.py:100-103, test_cookie_auth_reauths_on_401).

title: Rate limiting & retry logic
description: Respect per-tracker rate limits, implement exponential backoff and retry on transient errors. Part of Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf).
close_reason: Verified done. Delivered via Task 6 (3z5). TokenBucket per-tracker rate limiting + exponential backoff/retry on 429/5xx (test_retries_on_429/500, test_raises_rate_limit_error_after_max_retries, test_does_not_retry_on_404).

title: [Epic] Core API Client
description: Foundation library for all Gazelle tracker interactions. Covers authentication, rate limiting, retry logic, full endpoint coverage (torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications), typed response models, and tracker-specific adapters for Orpheus and Redacted. All other feature epics depend on this.
close_reason: Epic complete. All four feature areas verified and closed: 41c (auth/session), z0v (rate limit/retry), 59a (endpoint coverage), 11h (Orpheus/RED adapters). Implementation shipped via closed Task 1-12 issues + nmq/6ue; full non-integration suite 42 passed on main (4b85d0b). Downstream epics 7gj (Cross-Seed) and p53 (Upload Monitoring) depend on this but are separate, not-yet-built scope.

title: torrents: add get_group() for torrentgroup action
description: Add TorrentResource.get_group(group_id) calling ajax.php action=torrentgroup, returning a TorrentGroup with all editions/torrents. Highest-value gap: complements existing torrents.get() (action=torrent), which only returns a single torrent with the group embedded. Add/extend TorrentGroup model + fixtures for both trackers.

title: [Epic] API surface completeness — remaining Gazelle ajax routes
description:
The Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf, closed) shipped a deliberate read-only subset: index, torrent, browse, artist, request(s), collage, inbox, notifications, download (~11 actions, ~38% of the documented surface). This epic tracks the remaining documented ajax.php actions from the OPSnet fork (the codebase Orpheus and RED descend from; docs: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md).

Missing READ actions: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum (main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker.

Missing WRITE actions: add_tag, add_log, request_fill/requestfill. (upload is owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1, not duplicated here.)

Per-tracker caveat: action sets diverge slightly between Orpheus and RED; logchecker/better are RED-flavored tooling. Verify each against live fixtures from both trackers where possible.

title: Set up MkDocs + Material + mkdocstrings documentation site
description: Add a rendered documentation site for the library using MkDocs, Material theme, and mkdocstrings (Python handler) to auto-generate API reference from type hints + docstrings. Includes docs dependency group, mkdocs.yml, reference pages, and a GitHub Pages CI workflow.
close_reason: MkDocs+Material+mkdocstrings site implemented, builds clean in --strict, Pages deploy workflow added. Merged to main.

title: Capture real API response fixtures from Orpheus and Redacted
description: Run devtools/capture_fixtures.py with a valid .env to populate tests/fixtures/orpheus/ and tests/fixtures/redacted/. Blocked on fixing the API key header format (see related bug). Fixtures are required for model tests (4.3/4.4) and will reveal tracker-specific schema divergences needed for task 4.2.
close_reason: Fixtures captured locally for both trackers (index, notifications, torrent, artist x2). Kept out of git by design (tests/fixtures/ gitignored — index responses contain passkey/authkey). capture_fixtures.py fixed for per-tracker auth + artist extraction. Divergences discovered and recorded on python-gazelle-6ue; the two failing Orpheus model tests are xfail pending 6ue.

title: Cross-seed: file list verification & .torrent generation
description: Compare local file lists against candidate release files to verify exact match, then produce a .torrent ready to add to a torrent client. Part of Cross-Seed epic (python-gazelle-7gj).

title: Cross-seed: API metadata matching
description: Query target tracker API by artist, album, format, year to find candidate releases for cross-seeding. Part of Cross-Seed epic (python-gazelle-7gj).

title: Upload monitoring callback/event model
description: Expose a callback/event interface so scripts can register handlers that fire on deletion or trump events. Part of Upload Monitoring epic (python-gazelle-p53).

title: Upload deletion/trump polling
description: Poll notifications and torrent endpoints to detect when user uploads are deleted or trumped. Part of Upload Monitoring epic (python-gazelle-p53).

title: [Epic] Cross-Seed
description: Find matching releases on a target tracker via API metadata match (artist, album, format, year) combined with local file list verification. Produces a .torrent ready to add to a torrent client. Depends on Core API Client epic.

title: [Epic] Upload Monitoring
description: Detect when user uploads are deleted or trumped on Orpheus/Redacted. Polls notifications and torrent endpoints, exposes a callback/event model so automation scripts can react (e.g., remove torrent from client). Depends on Core API Client epic.

title: transport: write path (POST + auth token/CSRF + write-error mapping)
description: Foundational transport capability for all mutation endpoints. Today GazelleTransport.request() is GET-only and the only POST is _login() (cookie auth) — there is no generic write path. Add: a POST-based request method (form/multipart bodies), auth token / CSRF (auth_key) handling required by write actions, and write-specific error mapping. Per-tracker divergence likely (Orpheus vs RED token mechanics) — verify against both. This is a prerequisite shared by write endpoints (python-gazelle-5m0) and cross-upload submission (python-gazelle-xn1); neither should reimplement it. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md

title: write endpoints: add_tag, add_log, request_fill
description: Add mutation endpoints: add_tag (tag a torrent group), add_log (attach rip log to a torrent), request_fill/requestfill (mark request filled). Builds on the transport write path (python-gazelle-9l5, blocking prerequisite) — do NOT reimplement POST/CSRF handling here. NOTE: action=upload is intentionally excluded — owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1.

title: forum: ForumResource (main, viewforum, viewthread)
description: New ForumResource covering the forum read actions: list categories (action=forum&type=main), threads in a forum (type=viewforum, paginated), and posts in a thread incl. polls (type=viewthread). New models for Category/Forum/Thread/Post. Larger than a single method — feature-sized.

title: site reads: top10 + announcements
description: Add site-level read endpoints. top10 (action=top10; ranked torrents/tags/users, param for type+limit) and announcements (action=announcements; site news/blog posts). New resource (e.g. SiteResource) + models.

title: artists: add similar() for similar_artists action
description: Add ArtistResource.similar(artist_id) calling ajax.php action=similar_artists, returning related artists. Small extension to the existing ArtistResource; reuse ArtistResult-style model.

title: personal lists: bookmarks + subscriptions
description: Add read access to the user's saved items. bookmarks (action=bookmarks, type=torrents|artists) and subscriptions (action=subscriptions, subscribed forum threads with unread status). New resource(s) + models. Both are auth'd, current-user endpoints.

title: user: profile-by-id, usersearch, user_torrents
description: Extend UserResource beyond me()/index. Add: get(user_id) -> User (action=user, public profile + community stats); search(query) (action=usersearch); torrents(user_id, type) (action=user_torrents). Reuse/extend the User model; profile-by-id omits private fields present in index.

title: Fix pre-existing ruff lint + format violations repo-wide
description:
Running 'uv run ruff check .' reports 24 errors and 'uv run ruff format --check .' wants to reformat 9 files. These are pre-existing (predate python-gazelle-6ue) and live in files outside that change, so they were left untouched. This task clears them so the repo passes the lint/format gate documented in CLAUDE.md.

Lint (24 errors, all auto-fixable via 'ruff check --fix'):
  - 19x I001 (import block unsorted/unformatted)
  - 5x F401 (unused import)
Affected: src/pygazelle/__init__.py, client.py, resources/{artists,base,collages,inbox,notifications,requests,torrents,user}.py, tests/integration/{conftest,test_orpheus,test_redacted}.py, tests/models/conftest.py, tests/{test_client,test_errors,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Format (9 files via 'ruff format'): devtools/capture_fixtures.py, src/pygazelle/{__init__,client,sync}.py, src/pygazelle/models/__init__.py, src/pygazelle/resources/__init__.py, tests/{test_client,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Caution: before blindly applying F401 auto-fix, confirm the 5 unused imports are not intended public re-exports (check __all__ in any affected __init__.py). Run the full non-integration suite after fixing to confirm no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: 'uv run ruff check .' reports 0 errors; 'uv run ruff format --check .' reports all files formatted; 'uv run pytest --ignore=tests/integration' still passes (currently 42); no intended public re-export removed.

title: Write real README replacing simple-modern-uv template placeholder
description: README.md was still the default template stub. Replace with real docs: intro/features, install, async quick-start, sync usage, authentication, resources table, rate limiting & retries, error hierarchy, and links to CLAUDE.md and docs/. Examples verified against the actual public API.
close_reason: Replaced template placeholder README with real library docs (usage, auth, resources, errors); examples verified against the public API.

title: Document build/test, architecture, conventions, and fixture/.env setup in CLAUDE.md
description: CLAUDE.md had empty placeholder sections. Fill Build & Test (uv/pytest/ruff/basedpyright/codespell commands + .env and capture_fixtures.py setup for skipping integration/model tests), Architecture Overview (transport/client/resources/models/sync/errors layers), and Conventions & Patterns (GazelleModel camelCase aliasing, adding endpoints, tracker divergences, style, live-API care).
close_reason: Filled Build & Test, Architecture Overview, Conventions & Patterns, and added fixture/.env setup guidance in CLAUDE.md.

title: Implement tracker-specific model subclasses for Orpheus/Redacted divergences
description: Spec requires tracker-specific Pydantic model subclasses where Orpheus and Redacted return divergent schemas. Cannot be implemented until real API fixtures are captured and schema differences are known. Blocked on fixture capture task. Once fixtures are available, identify divergent fields and introduce tracker-specific submodels.
notes: Implemented on branch worktree-pygazelle-6ue-tracker-divergences (commit be39740). Made base Torrent/Artist tolerant rather than adding subclasses — see rationale. Model tests now 5 passed (was 3 passed + 2 xfailed). Quality gates pass for changed files (ruff, basedpyright 0 errors, codespell). NOT yet merged; pending code review + finish-branch.
close_reason: Implemented: base Torrent/Artist models made tolerant of Orpheus/RED divergences (commits be39740, 4b85d0b). Merged to main. Model tests 5 passed.

title: Cross-upload: metadata schema mapping
description: Translate torrent group metadata between Orpheus and Redacted schemas (artist, album, year, tags, format, media, description). Part of Cross-Upload epic (python-gazelle-xg5).

title: Cross-upload: upload submission & duplicate detection
description: Submit mapped metadata via target tracker upload API. Check for existing releases on target tracker before uploading to avoid duplicates. Part of Cross-Upload epic (python-gazelle-xg5).

title: [Epic] Cross-Upload
description: Upload a release from one tracker (Orpheus/Redacted) to the other with correct metadata. Covers metadata schema mapping, upload form submission via API, and duplicate detection before uploading. Depends on Cross-Seed epic.

title: tooling reads: better + logchecker (RED-flavored)
description: Add the tooling read endpoints: better (action=better; single-seeded/transcode-candidate torrents) and logchecker (action=logchecker; validate ripping log quality). These are RED-flavored and may be absent/divergent on Orpheus — gate or mark Optional per the tracker-divergence convention. Lower priority/niche.

title: Commit sanitized fixtures so model tests run in CI
description: tests/fixtures/ is gitignored because real index.json responses contain passkey/authkey. As a result the model tests (tests/models/) skip in CI where no fixtures exist. Provide sanitized fixtures (redact passkey, authkey, announce URLs, and any account identifiers) that can be safely committed, so model parsing tests run in CI. Source data captured locally via devtools/capture_fixtures.py. Relates to python-gazelle-6ue (which fixes the model divergences those tests assert).

value: Gazelle ajax route coverage audit (2026-06): core API client (epic ahf, closed) implements ~11 of ~29 documented OPSnet-fork actions (~38%). IMPLEMENTED: index, torrent, browse, artist, request, requests, collage, inbox, inbox&viewconv, notifications, download. MISSING READ: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum(main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker. MISSING WRITE: add_tag, add_log, request_fill (upload owned by cross-upload epic xn1). Follow-ups tracked under epic python-gazelle-mca. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md. Transport currently only does read GETs + download — write endpoints need a POST/CSRF path first.

Tracker schema divergences (Orpheus vs RED) are handled by making the BASE pydantic models tolerant (Optional fields + union types like 'bool | str | None'), NOT by per-tracker subclasses. Reason: resources like TorrentResource.get always build the base model regardless of tracker, and tests/models validate both trackers' fixtures against the same base class. Example (python-gazelle-6ue): Torrent.info_hash/remastered made Optional (Orpheus omits), Torrent.free_torrent is 'bool | str | None' (Orpheus sends 'Normal' string enum, RED sends bool), Artist.tags is 'list[ArtistTag] | list[str]' (Orpheus sends {name,count} objects). Subclasses would be dead code unless the resource layer dispatches per tracker.
